title: Shell-ebrating Our Slow and Steady Friends
story: Turtles get their own special day on May 23 when people around the world celebrate World Turtle Day! This awesome holiday helps everyone learn about these amazing shelled creatures and how to protect them. People visit turtle rescue centers, share turtle photos on social media, and learn cool turtle facts. Some folks even dress up like turtles or eat turtle-shaped cookies. Schools often have special turtle lessons, and aquariums host turtle-themed events. It's the perfect day to appreciate these incredible animals that have been around since dinosaur times and remind everyone why we need to keep their homes safe!
activity_question: What do people often visit on World Turtle Day to learn about turtles?
bonus_fact: Some turtles can live over 100 years old!

title: Sweet and Stretchy Taffy Day!
story: Every May 23, candy lovers celebrate National Taffy Day by enjoying this chewy, stretchy treat! Taffy shops across the country offer special flavors and discounts, while families make homemade taffy by pulling and stretching the sugary mixture until it becomes perfectly chewy. Many people share photos of colorful taffy collections on social media, showing off flavors like strawberry, vanilla, and chocolate. Some candy stores even host taffy-pulling demonstrations where kids can watch the magical process of turning liquid sugar into delicious, bite-sized pieces of joy.
activity_question: What do people do to make homemade taffy?
activity_choices: Pull and stretch the sugary mixture
Freeze it in ice cube trays
Bake it in the oven for an hour
activity_answer: Pull and stretch the sugary mixture
bonus_fact: Salt water taffy doesn't actually contain salt water - it's just a name!

title: Jamaica's May Work Party Day
story: Every May 23, Jamaica celebrates Labour Day with a twist that makes work feel like a party! Instead of staying home, thousands of Jamaicans grab tools, paintbrushes, and cleaning supplies to spruce up their communities together. Schools get fresh coats of paint, parks become spotless, and neighborhoods shine brighter. It's called "beautification day" because everyone works side by side to make their island even more beautiful.
activity_question: What do Jamaicans typically do on Labour Day?
activity_choices: Work together to clean and improve their communities
Have a big barbecue and stay home
Go to the beach and relax
activity_answer: Work together to clean and improve their communities
bonus_fact: Some Jamaican schools compete to see who can make their campus look the most amazing!

title: Ships Ahoy for Maritime Day!
story: Every May 23, America celebrates National Maritime Day to honor the brave sailors and amazing ships that keep our oceans busy! People visit maritime museums, tour historic ships, and learn about life at sea. Some coastal cities throw festivals with boat parades and seafood feasts. It's the perfect day to appreciate how ships bring us everything from toys to bananas from faraway places!
activity_question: What do people often visit on National Maritime Day?
bonus_fact: The first steamship crossed the Atlantic Ocean in 1838, taking 15 days!
